macro_summary: Markets enter a peak earnings and macro event cluster with TSLA and GOOGL reporting tonight, MSFT and AAPL later this week, and NVDA in late August. The FOMC meets July 28-29, with CME FedWatch pricing a meaningful probability of a 25bps hike under Chair Warsh — creating a rare dual macro/earnings binary event on July 29 alone. AI memory and semiconductor stocks are leading a global risk-on rally, with South Korea's KOSPI AI bellwether up 4.6% overnight. Geopolitical risk in the Middle East (11 consecutive rounds of US-Iran strikes) is driving Brent crude to $94+, supporting a parallel energy sector rotation.
screening_note: 20 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold to qualify: 2 appearances. 7 tickers qualified. Conviction levels and cross-run consistency are noted in each idea.
key_events: Tonight (July 22 after close): TSLA + GOOGL earnings
Tonight (July 22 after close): SKHY Q2 2026 earnings
July 28-29: FOMC rate decision (2:00 PM ET July 29) — ~25% hike probability priced
July 29 after close: MSFT Q4 FY2026 earnings + Azure guidance
July 29 Seoul time: SKHY Q2 conference call
July 30 after close: AAPL fiscal Q3 FY2026 earnings
August 26 after close: NVDA Q2 FY2027 earnings
Ongoing: US-Iran military escalation, Hormuz disruption risk, Brent crude at $94.07

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 20 overnight screening runs — the strongest consensus signal of the session. The world's #1 HBM memory supplier debuted on Nasdaq July 10, 2026 at $149, surged to $194.80, then pulled back ~22% to ~$151, creating a technical re-entry near IPO support. Q2 2026 earnings print tonight after close with 100% of 2026 HBM/DRAM/NAND capacity reportedly sold out, CEO citing AI-driven supply shortage extending beyond 2030, and Barclays initiating Overweight with ~70% upside. Revenue already up 40.8% YoY and 58.3% QoQ.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings tonight (July 22 after close); Q2 conference call July 29 Seoul time
ideas.edge: Average analyst 12-month price target $281.67 vs. ~$151 current (~86% implied upside); Barclays cites ~70% upside on US ADRs; server DRAM prices expected +18% in Q3 2026; entire 2026 output sold out
ideas.bull_case: A Q2 beat tonight confirming sold-out capacity and raised forward guidance, combined with the Barclays initiation and fresh Nasdaq listing momentum, could rapidly close the 86% gap to analyst consensus targets as institutional ownership builds post-IPO.
ideas.bear_case: As a brand-new Nasdaq listing with very thin options liquidity and ±10% daily swings since debut, any earnings miss or softer-than-expected guidance could reprice sharply lower with limited hedging tools available; ADR premium/discount to KRX shares adds an additional volatility layer.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in 19 of 20 runs with 18 high-conviction votes — the strongest directional conviction in this digest. Trading at its cheapest forward multiple since 2023 (~21-22x NTM), Microsoft reports Q4 FY2026 on July 29 after close, the same session as the FOMC rate decision. Azure growth rate and FY2027 capex guidance are the swing variables; a strong beat would confirm AI-infrastructure durability and serve as a key read-through for NVDA. Morgan Stanley freshly initiated Overweight at $600 on July 21. Options (Aug expiry call spread) are strongly preferred over stock given the FOMC/earnings collision on July 29.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings July 29 after close; Morgan Stanley Overweight initiation at $600 (July 21)
ideas.edge: Consensus analyst price target ~$590-592 (97 analysts, mostly Strong Buy) with fresh $600 MS target implying ~20%+ upside; trading at cheapest forward multiple (21-22x NTM) since 2023; Q3 2026 revenue guidance set at $80.7B-$81.8B
ideas.bull_case: Azure acceleration above current consensus growth estimates plus raised FY2027 AI capex guidance would validate the AI-infrastructure supercycle thesis at a valuation well below historical norms, catalyzing multiple re-expansion toward the $600 target.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish FOMC surprise on the same day as the earnings print (July 29) creates dual downside pressure; any Azure deceleration or Copilot monetization miss at a still-premium absolute valuation could produce an outsized selloff given the event collision.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 17 of 20 runs and presents one of the most anomalous valuation setups in the digest: a forward P/E of ~22x near a decade low despite 212% EPS growth over 9 quarters, with 61 of 61 analyst Strong Buy ratings and a ~$97 gap to consensus price target. The August 26 earnings are the hard catalyst, but the MSFT print on July 29 serves as a near-term AI-capex read-through signal. Hyperscalers are projected to spend $600B+ in 2026 AI infrastructure capex, and the overnight KOSPI AI rally confirms global demand momentum. A Sep-expiry call or bull call spread allows time for the full catalyst chain (MSFT→AAPL→NVDA earnings) to play out with defined risk.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26 after close; MSFT capex read-through July 29; consensus EPS estimate revised up 6.69% in past 3 months
ideas.edge: Stock ~$206 vs. consensus price target $302.83 (~+47% gap); forward P/E ~22x near decade low; EPS estimates revised up 6.69% in 3 months; 61/61 analyst Strong Buy consensus
ideas.bull_case: A chain of strong AI-capex signals from MSFT (July 29), AAPL (July 30), and GOOGL into an August 26 NVDA print confirming $93.5B+ quarterly revenue would close the ~47% gap to consensus targets while validating the decade-low valuation as a structural mispricing.
ideas.bear_case: NVDA has meaningfully underperformed AMD (+70%) and MU (+45%) in H1 2026, suggesting some institutional rotation away; a hawkish FOMC or softer-than-expected hyperscaler capex guidance could further delay multiple re-expansion ahead of the August print.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 16 of 20 runs heading into a July 30 earnings print following a strong iPhone supercycle (Q2 iPhone sales +22%, total revenue +17% to $111.2B). A concurrent CXMT regulatory binary decision creates asymmetric headline risk. RSI is flagged at 82 (overbought) while the stock sits ~$284 — approximately $31 below the Wall Street consensus target of ~$315. A $100B buyback provides a valuation floor. Given the rich 37x P/E, an options structure (call spread or straddle/strangle) is the consensus preferred approach over outright stock.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings July 30 after close; concurrent CXMT chip supply regulatory decision
ideas.edge: Stock ~$284 vs. consensus target ~$315 (~11% gap); guided 14-17% revenue growth; $100B buyback backstop; options implied move ~5%+; RSI at 82 creates symmetric risk setup
ideas.bull_case: A services revenue beat above $31B/quarter combined with continued iPhone supercycle momentum and potential foldable iPhone announcement in fall would re-rate shares toward the $315+ consensus target with buyback activity supporting the floor.
ideas.bear_case: RSI at 82 signals overbought conditions entering a binary event; a miss at a 37x P/E multiple leaves little margin of safety, and an adverse CXMT regulatory ruling could simultaneously pressure supply chain and sentiment — compounded by the day-prior FOMC overhang.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust (Nasdaq-100 ETF)
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 8 of 20 runs, all with medium conviction, as a diversified vehicle to trade the unprecedented concentration of Mag-7 earnings and the FOMC decision into the same 5-day window (July 22-29). It captures the semis-led risk-on momentum (KOSPI-Nasdaq correlation at a 2-year high of 0.46) while avoiding single-stock binary risk. The dual use case is clear across runs: a call spread to ride the AI earnings wave, or a put spread/iron condor as a portfolio hedge against the ~25% probability FOMC hike surprise overlapping exactly with MSFT earnings on July 29.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 29 at 2:00 PM ET (same session as MSFT earnings); TSLA + GOOGL prints tonight July 22
ideas.edge: FOMC hold expected but ~25% hike probability creates meaningful binary tail risk; IV elevated into dual catalyst cluster; KOSPI-Nasdaq correlation at 0.46 (2-year high vs. 5-year avg 0.16); defined-risk spreads benefit from elevated premium environment
ideas.bull_case: A sweep of in-line or better Mag-7 prints this week combined with an FOMC hold confirmation on July 29 removes the two largest near-term overhangs simultaneously, likely driving a relief rally in QQQ toward and through recent highs.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish FOMC surprise (25bps hike) landing at 2:00 PM on the same afternoon as MSFT earnings would create a dual shock with no circuit breaker — QQQ would absorb the full force of both macro and earnings repricing simultaneously.

ideas.name: Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund
ideas.thesis: XLE appeared in 3 of 20 runs as the lone non-tech consensus idea, driven by an active US-Iran military escalation (11 consecutive rounds of strikes as of July 22) and Strait of Hormuz disruption risk pushing Brent to $94.07 (+3.4% today). The energy sector is up ~40% YTD vs. S&P 500 flat, with oil refiners up 80%+ YTD. XLE provides diversified, liquid exposure to XOM, CVX and energy majors as both a momentum trade and a geopolitical hedge against a tech-heavy portfolio.
ideas.catalyst: Ongoing US-Iran conflict with Secretary Rubio signaling stalled talks; Brent crude at $94.07 (1-month high as of July 22 close); ECB rate decision July 23
ideas.edge: Brent crude +3.4% today to $94.07; XLE +1.20% on the day vs. S&P -0.14% — clear sector rotation signal; energy sector up ~40% YTD, crack spreads at multi-decade highs; BlackRock bull case $150+ if Hormuz further disrupted
ideas.bull_case: Further Hormuz disruption or Iranian infrastructure strikes removing barrels from the market could send Brent toward $110-150 range in the BlackRock scenario, with XLE directly leveraging oil price appreciation across diversified integrated majors and refiners.
ideas.bear_case: A diplomatic breakthrough or ceasefire announcement would rapidly unwind the geopolitical risk premium built into oil — Goldman Sachs bear case targets $80 Q4 — and an FOMC rate hike could simultaneously pressure demand expectations and the broader commodity complex.

ideas.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
ideas.thesis: SPY appeared in exactly 2 of 20 runs — just at the minimum threshold — as a macro hedge instrument rather than a directional trade. Both runs focused on the July 29 FOMC decision as a near-coin-flip binary (CME FedWatch pricing ~25-46% hike probability depending on timing), with Bank of America projecting 3 more hikes in 2026 under hawkish Chair Warsh. VIX at 17 keeps options premium relatively cheap, making a short-dated put spread or collar a quantifiably attractive hedge against a hawkish surprise colliding with peak earnings week. Use as portfolio protection, not a standalone directional bet.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 29 at 2:00 PM ET; PCE inflation revised to 3.6%; Bank of America projects 3 more hikes in 2026
ideas.edge: VIX at 17 (options relatively cheap); S&P 500 at ~7,509 — a surprise hike could reprice 2-4% instantly; protective puts or collar on SPY is quantifiably cheap relative to the binary macro risk
ideas.bull_case: FOMC holds rates as consensus expects, clearing the largest near-term macro overhang and allowing the strong Mag-7 earnings momentum to drive SPY toward new highs with limited selling pressure.
ideas.bear_case: A surprise 25bps hike on July 29 simultaneously with MSFT earnings would represent an unprecedented dual shock — hawkish Fed pivot + potential earnings repricing — creating a rapid 2-4%+ drawdown with limited near-term recovery catalyst.
